page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-48 is a compute-focused virtual machine instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 180 GB of system memory. Powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8581C processor operating at 2.3 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server provides 24 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor is equipped with a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and has zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark testing, the c4-standard-48 demonstrates strong performance across multiple domains. Single-core and multi-core Geekbench scores rank in the top tier and strong categories respectively, with notable strengths in image processing and text processing. The instance features advanced hardware instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and Advanced Vector Extensions, which contribute to strong performance in Passmark extended instructions and encryption tests. Memory bandwidth tests show a strong read-only small block cached speed of 3389.0 GB/sec, though uncached large block read and write speeds drop to average levels.

This hardware configuration is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads that benefit from specialized instruction sets, such as software compilation, cryptography, and static web serving. The inclusion of AMX tiles enables strong performance in CPU-based machine learning tasks, as evidenced by strong LLM inference speeds for small and medium models. However, the lack of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPUs represents a tradeoff for workloads requiring high-speed local disk I/O or massive parallel graphics acceleration. It is best utilized for database operations, web serving, and general-purpose compute tasks that require a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 3.75 GB per vCPU.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-48 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs and 180 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz. With 24 physical cores and hyper-threading, the instance incorporates a 260 MB L3 cache and advanced instruction sets including Intel AMX and AVX-512. Benchmark results indicate strong performance in multi-threaded decompression, software compilation, and cryptography, alongside top-tier single-core Geekbench results for image and text processing. While the instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, its high memory bandwidth and dedicated compute resources make it highly efficient for database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-48 is an x86_64 server with 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 180 GB RAM, and advanced AMX/AVX-512 instruction sets, optimized for compute-heavy workloads and database operations.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-48 features 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 180 GB RAM, delivering strong performance for compute-intensive workloads.
tagline: A dedicated 48-vCPU compute instance featuring Intel Xeon processors and advanced matrix extensions for accelerated mathematical and database workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs and 180 GB RAM powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated cryptography and matrix operations.
Strong multi-threaded performance in software compilation and database operations.
Top-tier single-core benchmarks in image and text processing.
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the base configuration.
